Business Owner Introduction
ESOP as an Exit Option
Owner
{{ownerName}}
Company

Date

Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01The Question

Have you considered what happens to your business — and your employees — when you're ready to step back?

Most business owners spend years building something they care about. But when it comes time to exit, the options often feel limited: sell to a competitor, hand the keys to private equity, or shut the doors. There's another path that many owners don't know about — one that lets you cash out, protect your employees, and preserve what you've built.

§ 02What Is an ESOP?

An Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) is a qualified retirement plan that lets your employees gradually become owners of the company. You sell your shares to the ESOP trust, your employees build wealth over time, and the company continues to operate — often with stronger retention and motivation than before.

It's not a giveaway. Your employees don't buy shares out of their own pockets. The ESOP trust borrows money to purchase your shares, and the company repays that loan over time using pre-tax dollars. It's a win for you, a win for your employees, and a win for the company's long-term stability.

§ 03Why Owners Choose ESOPs
A.Legacy preservation. Your company keeps its name, its culture, and its people. No layoffs, no rebranding, no dismantling what you spent decades building.
B.Tax advantages. Under IRC §1042, you can defer capital gains tax on the sale proceeds if you reinvest in qualified replacement securities. That can mean significantly more money in your pocket compared to a traditional sale.
C.Employee retention. Employee-owners stay longer, work harder, and care more about the company's success. ESOP companies have 25% higher median tenure than comparable non-ESOP firms.
D.Gradual transition. You don't have to walk away overnight. Many owners stay involved during a multi-year transition, mentoring the next generation while enjoying the financial benefit of the sale.
§ 04How Forhemit Helps

Forhemit is a California Public Benefit Corporation dedicated to ESOP transition stewardship. We guide business owners through every step of the process — from initial evaluation and valuation through financing, legal structuring, closing, and the years that follow.

Our role is to make the complex manageable. We coordinate the team of specialists your transaction needs (trustees, ERISA counsel, appraisers, lenders), keep the process on track, and make sure you understand every decision before you make it.

Our promise: We will never rush you into a decision. Our job is to give you clear, honest information so you can choose what's best for you, your family, and your employees.
§ 05No Pressure

This letter is educational, not a sales pitch. We reach out to business owners who may be good candidates for an ESOP because many don't know the option exists — and by the time they learn about it, they've already committed to another path.

If an ESOP isn't right for you, that's perfectly fine. We'd rather you know your options now than wish you'd explored them later.

§ 06Next Steps

If you're curious — even a little — we'd love to talk. A 15-minute conversation is all it takes to learn whether an ESOP might be a fit for your situation.

No commitment, no obligation, and everything you share stays confidential.
